heading: Professional Journey
subtitle: Roles where I turned messy data into decisions across research, analytics, and machine learning.

positions.title: Research Assistant
positions.description: Building Python image-analysis and unsupervised ML pipelines to cluster biological samples and separate technical imaging artifacts from real biological variation.
achievements: Developed a data analysis image analysis pipeline that processed over 91 biological samples using Python (scikit-learn, NumPy, Pandas) and extracted 144-dimensional features from color histograms, texture (LBP), and edges, which streamlined the workflow and enabled rapid identification of sample differences.

Applied unsupervised machine learning techniques including K-means clustering, PCA, and t-SNE visualization, achieving 0.38 silhouette score and identifying distinct visual patterns across sample categories.

Conducted statistical correlation analysis distinguishing technical artifacts from biological variation, determining that 80% of observed differences stemmed from imaging conditions rather than morphological factors.

positions.title: Graduate Engineer
positions.description: Captured and validated multimodal datasets for AR/VR computer-vision ML training, keeping ground-truth data clean, accurate, and audit-ready.
achievements: Captured and validated high-fidelity multimodal datasets for AI/ML training, organized data-quality metrics in Excel, and maintained 98% accuracy across high-volume workflows.

Performed data-integrity checks and resolved anomalies on structured datasets using SQL queries, database tools, and Excel-based validation, delivering clean, consistent, ML-ready ground-truth outputs for Meta’s AR/VR computer-vision systems.

Supported pipelines by automating ETL uploads and validation logging with Python scripts, documenting collection sessions, and generating data visualization reports that provided audit-ready datasets for ML development.

positions.title: Data Scientist (Capstone)
positions.description: Built an AWS and Python analytics pipeline for sustainability datasets, with forecasting models and Tableau dashboards to guide stakeholder decisions.
achievements: Built an end-to-end AWS and Python analytics pipeline to automate ingestion, validation, and forecasting across 500+ organizations’ sustainability datasets using Excel and ETL processes, improving data processing efficiency by 35% and reducing manual effort.

Developed regression and time-series models and applied data visualization techniques to identify key sustainability performance drivers, enabling stakeholders to prioritize high-impact initiatives and supporting data-driven decision-making at scale.

Designed Tableau dashboards that tracked KPIs and performance trends using SQL to extract and transform data, worked with cross-functional teams to verify data accuracy, and set up structured data-collection workflows and quality-control checks; this cut reporting time, letting stakeholders receive insights faster and improve decisions.

positions.title: Data Analyst Intern
positions.description: Analyzed financial spend data with SQL, Python, and Tableau to surface cost savings and improve budget forecasting for finance leadership.
achievements: Analyzed multi-year financial spend data using SQL, Python, and Excel, using ETL processes to uncover $80K+ in cost-saving opportunities and developing regression-based forecasting models that improved annual budget planning accuracy by 15%.

Built automated dashboards using Tableau and maintained bill of materials integrations for tracking budget KPIs, variance trends, and forecasting metrics, reducing manual reporting workload by 15% and improving real-time visibility for finance leadership.

Partnered with finance leadership to translate analytical findings into actionable allocation strategies, validating assumptions through statistical testing and delivering data-driven strategic insights that supported informed budget decision-making.
